title: Syracuse Inner Harbor is no place for a concrete plant (Editorial Board Opinion)
summary: Editorial board opposes Heidelberg Materials' plan to restart a concrete batch plant at 411-421 Spencer Street, two blocks from the Syracuse Inner Harbor and the $100M aquarium under construction. The board argues the 24/7 operation is incompatible with waterfront redevelopment goals and recommends the Common Council deny the special permit application. County Executive Ryan McMahon has offered to help relocate the facility.

political_signal.description: Editorial board publicly opposes concrete plant permit; positions decision as protecting $85M county aquarium investment and waterfront redevelopment vision. County Executive aligned in opposition.

processing_notes: Dollar amounts: $100M (aquarium), $85M (county funding), millions (developer rehab investment) — validate precision
Application withdrawn twice from planning board agenda — suggests political/procedural maneuvering; escalation risk
Common Council has final permit authority — key decision point for CIO tracking
